input.message: Plan created successfully at docs/plans/semantic-routing.md. The plan covers message routing for unthreaded Telegram conversations with three implementation phases.
I've finished writing the plan document. It includes all required sections: Documentation, Update System, and Agent Integration. The tracking issue is #412.
Build complete. All 4 builder tasks finished successfully. Created scripts/new_feature.py with 340 lines, updated config/settings.py, and added 3 new test files. Moving to test phase next.
Starting implementation now. Reading the plan document and setting up the worktree.
Progress update: implemented the core data model and API endpoints. Still need to write tests, run review, and create documentation.
Code changes committed. 5 files modified, 2 new files created. All ruff checks pass. No tests written yet.

Build output:
- scripts/feature.py: 200 lines
- tests/test_feature.py: 150 lines
- config update: added 3 new settings

All unit tests passing (12/12). Integration tests not run yet. PR not created.
PR #45 created successfully: https://github.com/tomcounsell/ai/pull/45. All tests passing. Review looks clean. Still need to run documentation cascade.
Test results: 45/45 unit tests passing, 8/8 integration tests passing. Ruff format clean. Ruff check clean. Moving forward with the pipeline.
Fixed the failing test by correcting the mock setup. All 3 test files now pass. Review stage is next in the pipeline.

All pipeline stages complete. PR #45 merged. Documentation updated. Plan migrated.

Summary:
- Feature implemented and tested
- PR: https://github.com/tomcounsell/ai/pull/45
- All checks passing
- Docs created at docs/features/new-feature.md

I see two possible approaches for the database migration:

Option A: Add a new column with a default value and backfill
Option B: Create a new table and migrate data in batches

Should I use approach A or B? Option A is simpler but locks the table during migration.

Fatal error during build:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "scripts/build.py", line 45, in main
    result = api_client.deploy(config)
  File "lib/api.py", line 112, in deploy
    raise ConnectionError("Cannot reach deployment server at staging.example.com")
ConnectionError: Cannot reach deployment server at staging.example.com

This is an infrastructure issue I cannot resolve. The staging server appears to be down.

The integration test requires an API key for the external service. I found a reference to EXTERNAL_SERVICE_KEY in the test file but it's not set in the environment. Is this the right key to use, or should I create a test mock instead?
Hey, what's the weather like? I was just curious about the forecast for this weekend.

I need database admin access to run the migration script. The current credentials only have read permissions. I've tried three different connection strings and none have write access. This is blocking the entire build.
The capital of France is Paris. It's located in the north-central part of the country along the Seine River. Is there anything else you'd like to know?
